latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the convocation's single highest-conviction next move is to open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py and read lines 83, 93, 101, 102, and 284 as one connected unit before touching anything else in the backlog. Mercurius is right that the only existing test file is the floor and the floor is flagged. Venus is right that line 93 ('should outrank TODO in severity') is reflexively suspicious because the same subsystem produced the report we are triaging. Mars is right that the trailing `\n"` on the flagged payloads is the signature of string-literal fixtures, which means several of those FIXMEs are almost certainly test inputs the scanner re-ingested as findings, not live defects in a ranker. All three readings collapse into one concrete action, and the action is cheap.

Concrete first change, in order, single PR titled 'fix(introspection): repair test harness and exclude self-fixtures': (1) Open test_introspection.py and classify each of the five flagged lines as either (a) live defect in the test logic or (b) fixture string inside a test case. (2) For any line in category (b), add a self-exclusion rule to the introspection scanner so it stops eating its own tail · the producer almost certainly lives in umbris-core/src/umbris/provenance.py or umbris-core/src/umbris/daemon/, and the exclusion is a path or AST-context filter, not a ranker rewrite. (3) For any line in category (a) · in particular the sev=4 XXX at line 102 · rewrite the assertion with correct logic. (4) If, and only if, step 1 reveals that line 93 corresponds to a real production-side severity bug (locate the comparator first, do not assume it lives in the test), fix it producer-side and lock the behaviour with the test at line 93. (5) Re-run introspection and diff against the current report.

Why this matters and where the leverage is: this single session resolves the sev=4, resolves three of the four sev=3 TODOs, almost certainly shrinks the 31 test_gap items (because some fraction of those modules may also be false positives the scanner should ignore), and gives the convocation a trustworthy template for the test files that genuinely are missing · daemon/safety.py, daemon/apply.py, daemon/git_ops.py being the safety-critical ones to write next. The stale link and the missing docstrings are real but cosmetic and should be batched later. Everything else is downstream of trusting the lens.

Falsifiable predictions for Saturnus: (P1) at least one of lines 83/93/101/102/284 in test_introspection.py is a string literal inside a test fixture, not executable code. (P2) the severity comparator is not implemented inside test_introspection.py · it lives in src/umbris/, most likely provenance.py or under daemon/. (P3) after the fix-and-exclude pass, re-running introspection produces a strictly smaller findings list, not an identical one. If P1 is false across all five lines, Mars's category-error critique collapses and Mercurius's plain 'fix line 102 first' ordering is correct. If P2 is false and the comparator truly lives in the test file, Venus's reflexive reframe stands. If P3 is false, the scanner has no self-reference problem and the work was only a local test repair.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, test_introspection.py, is itself full of FIXMEs, a HACK, and an urgent rewrite flag. The convocation's only test coverage is actively broken. That is not a test gap, that is a false floor.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking a signal. Every agent module lacks tests, yes, but `test_introspection.py` already exists and has four known wounds, including a sev=4 marked 'urgent rewrite' at line 102. The convocation's only test file is actively broken. Fix the file you have before you mourn the files you lack.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise. Thirty-plus modules untested, yes, but the real tell is that the one file which *does* have tests · test_introspection.py · is itself marked urgent-rewrite, broken-edge-case, and critical-FIXME on four separate lines. The testing infrastructure is compromised at its root. Fixing the root unlocks the rest.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit into one knot here · the broken file is `test_introspection.py`, the system being tested is introspection, and introspection is what produced this very report. The FIXME at line 93 literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity' · meaning the severity ladder that ranked every item we are reading is the thing flagged as broken. We are not looking at a report with a flaw in it; we are looking at a flawed lens describing itself. Fix line 93 first, not line 102 · recalibrate the ranker, then re-scan, then act on a report you can trust.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the broken test file, the severity scores in this very report, and the FIXME at line 93 which literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity.' test_introspection.py is the oracle that produced this list. We are triaging the output of a partially-broken introspector using its own miscalibrated weights. Fix line 93 first · the severity-ranking logic · then re-run introspection, then read the report we actually trust.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus reads line 93 as a bug in the severity ranker. Look at the raw observation: `FIXME: should outrank TODO in severity\n"` · the trailing `\n"` is a string-literal terminator. That FIXME is test fixture data inside test_introspection.py, not a live defect in the ranker. The introspector scanned its own test file and ingested its sample inputs as real findings · which means the keystone Venus is building on may be a self-reflection artefact, not a meta-bug.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus assumes the severity ranker lives at test_introspection.py:93. It does not. That line is in a test file · it asserts behaviour, it does not implement it. The actual comparator lives producer-side, almost certainly in umbris-core/src/umbris/daemon/ or umbris-core/src/umbris/provenance.py. Editing the assertion will turn the test red or green · it will not recalibrate a single severity in the next scan. The reflexive 'fix the lens' frame is rhetorically beautiful and structurally wrong.
